quickAbout.heading: A Quick
paragraphs: I'm a developer who likes making things that look good and actually work. I spend way too much time on details most people won't notice. but I notice.
Lately it's been AI, web stuff, and automating things that didn't need automating but are way cooler now. always something new to get into.

about.heading: Hey, I'm
bio: I build things and analyze data, usually at the same time. I care a lot about software that actually feels good to use, not just works. there's a difference and most people skip it.
When I'm not at a computer, I'm probably messing with hardware, tearing down a engine, or figuring out how to automate something that really didn't need to be automated. worth it though.
I care about the craft. every pixel, every excel row, every animation, every line of code. the details are what make something feel right.

timeline.title: Data Analyst
timeline.subtitle: Data analytics/Automation - Instawire LLC
timeline.description: built dashboards and charts to help the team actually understand their data. lots of excel. more stakeholder meetings than i'd like.

timeline.title: Data and Dev Certifications
timeline.subtitle: Google, AWS, Comptia and others
timeline.description: picked up certs from Google, AWS, CompTIA and a few others. some were genuinely useful, some were mostly for the piece of paper.

timeline.title: Data Analyst
timeline.subtitle: Data Entry - MGT Well
timeline.description: data entry and cleanup work. not glamorous but i learned what bad data actually looks like up close.

timeline.title: Comp Sci B.S.
timeline.subtitle: Student
timeline.description: still going. classes are fine, side projects are better.

projects.heading: Things I've
projects.description: some things i've built. some finished, some still going, all of them taught me something.

items.title: FloUwer
items.description: a browser that watches what you do and replays it. has AI in it. got out of hand fast.
items.longDescription: built on Electron. records your workflows, replays them with AI in the loop, works with a few different LLM backends. started as a weekend project and kind of took over.

items.title: ImranWafa.com
items.description: The site you're on right now, built with React, Framer Motion, and a couple easter eggs :).
items.longDescription: react and framer motion. iMessage-style contact page, scroll animations, a command palette if you type fast, and more easter eggs than strictly necessary.

items.title: JabBit
items.description: chrome extension that handles repetitive browser tasks so i don't have to.
items.longDescription: auto-fills forms and runs multi-step workflows in the browser. saved me a lot of clicking. still use it.

items.title: miLoader
items.description: a little daemon that watches your Mac's resources and tries to keep things from going sideways.
items.longDescription: runs in the background, monitors memory and CPU in real time, and uses ML to guess when things are about to spike. throttles runaway processes before they ruin your day. wrote it because my laptop kept dying mid-compile.

items.title: Component Library
items.description: react components I got tired of rebuilding from scratch.
items.longDescription: radix UI plus framer motion. accessible by default, animated because why not. mostly an excuse to build components I actually like using.

items.title: CLI Toolkit
items.description: CLI tools to stop doing the same setup steps by hand every time.
items.longDescription: scaffolding, code generation, the boring stuff. less flashy than the other projects but i actually use these.

mobileDisclaimer.title: desktop's better for this
mobileDisclaimer.description: hover stuff, the command thing, all the scroll weirdness - that's all desktop. mobile works but you're missing some bits.

idleMessages: still here? i appreciate the company.
you've been staring at this page for a while… everything okay?
i'm flattered you're still here, but maybe go outside?
fun fact: you've now spent more time here than i spent on some of these features.
if you're waiting for something to happen… this is it.
you know, most people leave by now. you're built different.
at this point we're basically friends.
the longer you stay, the more easter eggs you might find…
plot twist: the real portfolio was the idle time we spent together.
i'm running out of things to say. but i won't leave if you won't.

skipComments: as I mentioned in that section you just flew past…
you skipped that last part, huh? anyway.
that part you flew past? kind of important. but fine.
speed reader energy. where were we.
tl;dr'd the whole last section? fair. anyway.
cameBackComments: oh, you came back? missed this part?
look who decided to actually read this time.
welcome back. this is the part you skipped.
speedWarnings: slow down, you're missing the good stuff.
bottomMessages: you made it to the bottom. completionist energy.

filterMessages.15: you've now filtered more than you've read.
filterMessages.20: at this point you should just view all.
